zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Somerville於1305年出生在英國斯塔福爾維其諾時，她的父親Philip Somerville27歲，母親Margaret de Pype30歲。她於1325年與Sir Rhys Griffith I在威爾士卡馬特史爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1376年10月8日在英國瓦維其可爾斯托克頓享年71歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Somerville于1305年出生在英国斯塔福爾維其諾时，她的父亲Philip Somerville27岁，母亲Margaret de Pype30岁。她于1325年与Sir Rhys Griffith I在威尔士卡馬特史爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1376年10月8日在英国瓦維其可爾斯托克頓享年71岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Somerville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Graveron-Sémerville in Eure (Normandy), from the ancient Germanic personal name Sigimar (see Siemer ) + Old French ville ‘settlement’. The surname was taken to Scotland in the 12th century by William de Somerville, a retainer of David, Earl of Huntingdon, who became King David I of Scotland.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Somacháin (see Summers ) or Mag Shamradháin (see Somers ).

variants.name: Siemer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Helmuth, Otto, Theresia.
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Siegmar, composed of the elements sigi ‘victory’ + mari, meri ‘famous’.

variants.name: Summerville
meanings: English, Scottish (Lanarkshire), and Irish: variant of   Somerville  . This form of the surname is particularly common in Ireland.

variants.name: Simrell
meanings: Scottish: perhaps related to the Ulster nameSumeril, a variant of Somerville .

variants.name: Summerfield
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called with Old English sumor ‘summer’ + feld ‘field, open land’, denoting summer pasture or fields cultivated only in summer, such as Summerfield in Woodnesborough (Kent), Somerfield Court in Sellindge (Kent), and Summerfield in Marlborough (Wiltshire).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Sommerfeld  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Somerville was born in 1305, in Wichnor, Staffordshire, England, her father, Philip Somerville, was 27 and her mother, Margaret de Pype, was 30. She married Sir Rhys Griffith I in 1325, in Carmarthenshire, Wales. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 8 October 1376, in Stockton, Warwickshire, England, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Somerville est née en 1305, à Wichnor, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Philip Somerville, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Pype, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Rhys Griffith I en 1325, à Carmarthenshire, Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 8 octobre 1376, à Stockton, Warwickshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 71 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Somerville(이)가 1305년 영국 스태퍼드실르 위ch너에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Philip Somerville(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Pype(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1325년 웨일스 카마테실르에서 Sir Rhys Griffith I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1376년 10월 8일 영국 워윅실르 스톡턴에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Somerville nasceu em 1305, em Wichnor, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Philip Somerville, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Pype, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Rhys Griffith I em 1325, em Carmarthenshire, Gales. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 8 de outubro de 1376, em Stockton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, com 71 anos.

ru.Crossingtonshire, Barrow-upon-Soar, Leic: барроу упон соар, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Somerville родилась в 1305 г. в местности вичнор, стаффордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Philip Somerville было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Pype, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Rhys Griffith I в 1325 г. в местности кармартеншир, Уэльс. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 8 октября 1376 г. в местности стокктон, варвиккшир, Англия в возрасте 71 год.
